title: Perymenium sonoranum

The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, likely due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Perymenium sonoranum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects.
